name: Stuffed Chicken Florentine
recipeIngredient: 5.25 ounces Idaho sour cream with chives potatoes
recipeInstructions:
1. Remove any foil from the spinach package. Pierce the box several times.
2. Place the box on a paper towel and cook on High for 3 to 4 minutes, or until thawed.
3. When cool, squeeze out all liquid. Meanwhile, in a 2-quart microwave-safe casserole, combine potatoes, water, and a dash of nutmeg. Cover loosely with plastic wrap and cook on High for 8 to 10 minutes, stirring once.
4. Drain any remaining liquid. Stir in the prepared sauce mix and half of the drained spinach.
5. Loosen the chicken skin slightly, but do not remove it completely, to form a pocket. Spoon the potato mixture into the pocket, pulling the skin back into place to cover the potatoes. Secure with a wooden toothpick if necessary.
6. In a microwave-safe oblong baking dish, arrange the chicken in a single layer. In a small microwave-safe dish, melt butter and stir in paprika. Brush the chicken with the melted butter mixture.
7. Cover loosely with waxed paper and cook on High for 10 to 12 minutes, or until the chicken is tender (rotate the dish once).
8. Let stand for 5 minutes. Meanwhile, in a microwave-safe 4-cup measuring cup, combine milk with a dash of nutmeg, the remaining sauce mix, and the remaining spinach. Cook on High for 3 to 5 minutes, or until the sauce thickens, stirring once.
9. Serve the sauce over the chicken. Makes 6 servings.
